steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Cache Key Identity · Theory Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3831 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cache Key Identity contract for the supplied data.
